summary: Russian FPV drones struck a group near Chasiv Yar during the ceasefire, killing three soldiers later identified as Russian troops hit by their own side during an evacuation attempt. The "Rubicon" Center's air defense crews used FPV drones to intercept over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with more than 75% of intercepts involving 12 specific models. Ukrainian forces tested the ceasefire by dressing captured Russian POWs in neutral clothing and sending them out as a fake evacuation group, which was immediately hit by Russian FPV drones, killing all three. In other incidents, FPV drones hit two Russian soldiers on motorcycles assaulting Ukrainian positions, and a Russian soldier pretending to surrender was killed by FPV drones after attempting to flee. Ukrainian soldiers survived a Russian FPV drone attack near Kostiantynivka, with one holding a drone detector, and a nighttime return under tactical red light was interrupted by an enemy FPV drone strike on a "Shield" unit vehicle, which saved the crew.
